title: conyza bonariensis
intro: Conyza bonariensis, also known as flavellin or horseweed, is a perennial herb belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly for its flavonoids and terpenoids content. The health benefits of Conyza bonariensis include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Conyza bonariensis can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its use and efficacy may vary depending on the region and preparation method.
